alerts.title: Trump orders new strikes on Iran oil infrastructure; threatens seizure of Kharg Island
alerts.summary: President Trump ordered continued U.S. strikes on Iranian targets and explicitly threatened to seize Kharg Island and take "total control" of Iran's oil industry. Multiple sources (PBS, CNBC, The Guardian, AP News) confirm threat statements issued June 11. Strikes clustered around Persian Gulf oil infrastructure (31.7-32.2°N, 52.7-53.3°E) with intent to coerce Iranian capitulation on nuclear deal terms.
alerts.region: Iran, Persian Gulf, Strait of Hormuz

alerts.title: Iran strikes Muwaffaq Salti airbase in Jordan with 12 ballistic missiles; F-35s targeted
alerts.summary: IRGC confirmed targeting of Muwaffaq Salti (Al-Azraq) airbase in Jordan with 12 ballistic missiles on June 11, reportedly destroying hangar facilities housing F-35, F-16, and F-15 fighter jets, plus command-and-control center. Multiple Telegram sources (ME Spectator, Rybar) confirm visual impacts and Iranian claims of high effectiveness. Represents regional escalation beyond Iran-Israel axis into U.S. ally territory.

alerts.title: U.S. launches 49 Tomahawk missiles at Iranian targets in overnight strike
alerts.summary: Israeli Channel 12 media confirmed U.S. military conducted overnight strike on Iranian targets with 49 Tomahawk cruise missiles on June 11. Telegram sources (ME Spectator) corroborate missile count and overnight timing. Represents sustained dual-axis bombardment continuing second consecutive day of kinetic escalation without ceasefire restoration.

alerts.title: Iran uranium enrichment holds at 60% U-235; IAEA demands stockpile transparency amid kinetic war
alerts.summary: IAEA confirms Iran maintains uranium enrichment at 60% U-235 with breakout window of 1-2 weeks concurrent with active U.S.-Iran strikes and collapsed ceasefire (per global_metrics, incidents feeds). IAEA board passed resolution demanding Iran report stockpiles and grant inspector access. Dual-track crisis creates unprecedented proliferation risk during active hostilities.

alerts.title: U.S. sentiment sharply negative at 53%; Trump dominance in discourse signals political escalation support
alerts.summary: U.S. sentiment analysis shows 53% negative vs. 42% positive sentiment (5% neutral) with "Trump" appearing 58 times in top word frequency—far above baseline. Emergence of "Kharg Island" (10 mentions) and oil-control framing indicates domestic political narrative shift toward resource-seizure justification. Sentiment swing reflects public opinion tracking operational escalation trajectory.

analysis_note: The U.S.-Iran conflict has entered a second full day of sustained kinetic escalation with Trump personally directing strikes on Iranian oil infrastructure and explicitly threatening territorial seizure, while Iran retaliates against regional U.S. allies in Jordan and the Gulf, all amid a 60% uranium enrichment threshold and 1-2 week nuclear breakout window.
